what_it_does: Declares a module-level ATTACHMENT_TYPE dict registering 'board_items' — noticeboard items (requests/issues/tips/guides/ideas) attachable to a channel. Specifies id, label, target_kind, multi flag, and description.
observations: ATTACHMENT_TYPE keys: id ('board_items'), label ('Board items'), target_kind ('address'), multi (True), desc.
Inline comment on target_kind explains the target is board://<id>, opaque, resolved by stripping the scheme then calling cc_board.get_item.
multi: True indicates a channel can have many board-item attachments.
desc: 'a noticeboard item (board://<id>) bound to a channel — the channel's requests/issues/etc.'
Single-line module docstring summarizing the same intent.
standouts: Defines an address-scheme contract: board://<id> resolved via cc_board.get_item.
target_kind 'address' (opaque reference) rather than an inline/embedded target.
conventions: Seed-as-data dict pattern, same family as the ai_tics seed files but with attachment-type schema (target_kind/multi).
Uses an inline comment to encode the resolution protocol for the address scheme.
notes: A registry seed declaring the 'board_items' channel-attachment type bound to board://<id> addresses resolved through cc_board.
questions: What is the full set of valid attachment target_kind values besides 'address'?
Where is the board:// scheme parser / cc_board.get_item defined?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — declares the board_items attachment type exactly as the docstring states.
apparent_themes: channel attachments as a typed registry
opaque address references (board://<id>) over embedded content
intention_signals: Registers a way to attach many noticeboard items to a channel via opaque board addresses, keeping the channel decoupled from item storage.
novelty: An address-scheme attachment contract (board://<id> with strip-scheme resolution) declared as plain registry data.
contribution: Contributes the 'board_items' attachment-type definition and its board:// address resolution contract to a fused attachment-type registry.
summary_for_embedding: A Python seed file declaring the 'board_items' channel-attachment type targeting opaque board://<id> addresses resolved via cc_board.get_item.
intention_for_embedding: To register noticeboard items as a multi-valued, address-referenced attachment type bindable to a channel.
